disclaimer: Education/research only. Five-signal composite (scenario, rubric, risk, MC, DCF) — not a buy list. Confirm material facts in filings.

selection_memo.method: Five-signal composite: pool = top 60 by weighted percentile blend (scenario, rubric, risk, Monte Carlo, DCF from universe_composite_rank.csv; weights in shortlist_weights.json), then rubric total and US>EU>London tie-break. Theme caps fill to ≥20, then expand to ≤28 while valuation tier ≥ 1 when rank file present. Borda lens ranks kept for comparison. Anchors ['NVDA'] may enter pool below cutoff or swap in when rubric ≥18. Theme-seat (quantum): if that sleeve’s cap ≥1 and no quantum name is selected yet, the best in-pool quantum in consensus order (rubric ≥8) may replace the weakest non-anchor name.
selection_memo.why_not_top_20_by_rubric_alone: A single rubric total hides trade-offs between growth, quality, valuation scenario, risk, and distribution. The five-signal composite percentile blend forces names to score across models before theme caps apply.
counterarguments: Scenario, DCF, and Monte Carlo are correlated — weights spread but do not eliminate overlap.
Percentile ranks are relative to this universe snapshot, not absolute quality.
Tickers missing any universe signal row are excluded from composite (see fi_composite_universe_rank WARN).
Theme caps can block a high composite name in a full bucket.
US-listing tie-break is a liquidity preference, not a macro view.
selection_memo.what_to_watch_for: Compare cap_casualties and high_yoy_outside_pool: if a strategically important name appears there, consider manual overrides or widening the pool for the next pass.

deep_dive.what_company:
Diodes Incorporated, together with its subsidiaries, provides semiconductor products in Asia, the Americas, and Europe. The company offers MOSFET and SiC MOSFET discrete semiconductor products; data line protection, power line protection, thyrister, USB Type-C protection, and transient voltage suppressor protection devices; Schottky diodes, small signal switching diodes, Zener diodes, and SiC diodes; and bridges, super barrier rectifiers, Schottky rectifiers, Schottky bridge rectifiers, and fast and ultra-fast rectifiers. It also provides avalanche transistors, gate driver transistors, and pre-bias transistors; power management devices, such as AC-DC and DC-DC converters, digital isolators and isolated gate drivers, USB power switches, low dropout, photocoupler, and linear voltage regul…

It sits in the AI infra & compute sleeve of this research pack. Revenue up 15% year-on-year.
